items.title: Hong Kong Begins Trial Operation of New Gold Clearing System
items.description: Hong Kong has launched a trial operation of a new gold clearing system, backed by several major banks, in a key step toward its ambition to become a major bullion-trading hub with price-setting power.

items.title: Wall Street Warms to India’s Markets as Oil Pressures Fade
items.description: After spending more than a year on the sidelines, global investors are showing fresh interest in India as sentiment begins to improve across the country’s markets.

items.title: Hanwha Ocean Plunges Over 20% After Losing Canada Submarine Deal
items.description: Shares of Hanwha Ocean fell more than 20% on Tuesday morning after Canada picked a German bid to replace its aging submarine fleet over the South Korean shipbuilder’s proposal.

items.title: Island Pharma Jumps After Emergency-Use Approval for Ebola Drug
items.description: Australia’s Island Pharmaceuticals Ltd. surged as much as 47% after saying its experimental antiviral galidesivir had secured regulatory and ethics approvals for emergency use in patients with the Bun

items.title: LG Energy Profit Misses Estimates as Slow EV Demand Hits
items.description: LG Energy Solution Ltd. reported preliminary second-quarter earnings that missed analyst estimates, as lackluster support for electric vehicles in key markets like the US failed to offset surging dema

items.title: Gold Steadies as Traders Look to Fed for Interest-Rate Outlook
items.description: Gold was little changed as traders awaited minutes from the Federal Reserve’s last meeting for fresh insights into the path for US interest rates.

items.title: JPMorgan Asset Bets Strong Yuan, Weak Won Trend Will Flip
items.description: JPMorgan Asset Management has trimmed its long yuan positions against the dollar, shifting its focus to higher yielding currencies, as the yuan’s outperformance begins to cool.

items.title: Samsung’s Soaring Profit Fails to Impress After AI Chip Rally
items.description: Samsung Electronics Co.’s quarterly profit surged 19-fold, but failed to impress investors accustomed to eye-catching growth from the suppliers of chips to the global AI boom.

items.title: Intel-Backed AI Chip and Software Maker Syntiant Files for IPO
items.description: Syntiant Corp., a company making semiconductors and software for artificial intelligence, filed for an initial public offering to tap investors’ enthusiasm for the technology.

items.title: Stocks Slip as Tech Weakness Weighs, Samsung Falls: Markets Wrap
items.description: Asian equities dropped as technology shares came under renewed selling pressure due to investors rotating into other corners of the market. Chip bellwether Samsung Electronics Co. fell after its resul

items.title: Oil Advances as Strike Against Tanker in Hormuz Highlights Risks
items.description: Oil gained following reports of attacks on vessels in the Strait of Hormuz, highlighting continued risks to traffic in the waterway.

items.title: Japan Drives Asia Offshore Bonds to Record, Surpassing China Era
items.description: Japanese borrowers drove quarterly issuance in Asia Pacific’s offshore bond market to a record, eclipsing the previous peak set during an era of Chinese corporate dominance.

items.title: Hamilton Lane Raises $3.8 Billion Fund Targeting Mid-Market Private Equity Deals
items.description: Erik Hirsch, Co-CEO at Hamilton Lane, discussed the firm's recent $3.8 billion fundraise aimed at co-investing alongside private equity in mid-market companies. Hirsch emphasized the focus on smaller

items.title: Rivian Offers 75 Million Shares to Meet US Energy Loan Terms
items.description: Rivian Automotive Inc. is offering to sell 75 million shares as the electric vehicle company seeks to fund equity contributions related to a US Department of Energy loan.

items.title: 【今朝の5本】仕事を始める前に読んでおきたい厳選ニュース
items.description: 世界トップ5目指すとMUFG社長、SKハイニックス米上場へ、米株見通し、サウジ原油値下げ、ホルムズ脱出へ

items.title: Test Your ETF Knowledge In Our IQ Test
items.description: Bloomberg's Joel Weber puts Scarlet Fu, Katie Greifeld, and Eric Balchunas to the test on "Bloomberg ETF IQ" in this week's edition of "IQ Test." (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: How to Get the Most Out of Your Kid’s Trump Account
items.description: Families with young kids can officially start putting money in a Trump Account as of July 4, 2026. But is this account right for you and your kid's savings goals and needs? Sarah Foster explains  (Sou

items.description: Comprehensive cross-platform coverage of the U.S. market close on Bloomberg Television, Bloomberg Radio, and YouTube with Romaine Bostick, Katie Greifeld, Carol Massar and Emily Graffeo. (Source: Bloo

items.title: KKR’s Private Credit Clients Slow Down Their Redemption Requests
items.description: Investors in a KKR & Co. retail private credit fund got back all of their requested cash in the second quarter, a sign that individual investors’ skittishness over the asset class may be easing.

items.title: Traders Are Most Positive on Dollar Since 2015 as Fed Hike Looms
items.description: Global traders have turned the most positive on the outlook for the dollar since 2015 as bets that borrowing costs will remain elevated for longer have fueled a monthlong rally in the US currency.
